sloka_835.meaning: by the brāhmaṇ offerer who is Brahma
by a person whose consciousness is absorbed in Brahma action

sloka_842.meaning: O Arjuna, best of the Kurus
even this world (or human plane of meagre pleasures)
How, then, will it be possible for him to attain

sloka_813.meaning: O Arjuna, subduer of the enemy
after the passage of a long time

sloka_816.meaning: O Arjuna, subduer of the enemy

sloka_844.meaning: O Arjuna, subduer of the enemy

sloka_846.meaning: though appearing in the forms of their various material designations
all species from highest to lowest

sloka_825.meaning: knows Me in truth (as the Supreme Lord)

sloka_849.meaning: In this world, or, of the aforementioned practices of austerity, etc

sloka_853.meaning: by the sword of knowledge, sambandha-jñān

sloka_828.meaning: renunciation of action should be understood
Action prescribed (by the Vedas) should be understood
action prohibited (by the Vedas) should be understood

sloka_824.meaning: The four social divisions or varṇas brāhmaṇ, kṣatriya, vaiśya, and śūdra

sloka_838.meaning: Others (meditating mystic yogīs in realisation of the pure self [tvam])
the functions of the ten vital life-airs
into the yogic fire of self-purification

sloka_836.meaning: to the fire of Brahma (or to the Supersoul, Paramātmā, as the element tat, representing the sacrificial fire)
the self (jīvātmā, as the element tvam, representing the ghee of sacrifice)
by vibrating the praṇava mantra, Om

sloka_843.meaning: by the mouthpiece of the Vedas
to be born of action, in thought, word, and deed
you will be liberated from the bondage of action

sloka_819.meaning: dhyān (meditation), yajana (performance of sacrifice), paricharyā (worship), and saṅkīrtan (congregational chanting of the Holy Names) all centred on Me

sloka_833.meaning: beyond dualities such as heat and cold, pleasure and pain, attraction and aversion
in the event of either success or failure of action
satisfied with whatever is readily available

sloka_834.meaning: For one who is free from attachment

sloka_837.meaning: into the fires of the senses
into the fires of self (mental) control

sloka_840.meaning: bring the ascending air into union with the descending air while inhaling (pūraka)
by reducing their intake of food
the flow of both prāṇa and apāna while retaining the breath (kumbhaka)
the descending air into the ascending air while exhaling (rechaka)

sloka_830.meaning: and who has burnt all actions in the fire of knowledge

sloka_848.meaning: all karma (worldly actions and reactions in this life)

sloka_852.meaning: and overcome all his doubts by the cultivation of divine knowledge (sambandha-jñān, knowledge of the eternal serving relationship of the individual soul with the Lord)
one who has ceased all action in accordance with the path of sannyās, abnegation, by practising niṣkām karma-yoga, the path of selfless action
and who has realised the nature of the soul

sloka_821.meaning: by knowledge and austerities in relation to My service
wholeheartedly absorbed in services to Me
Free from mundane attachment, fear, and anger

sloka_845.meaning: endowed with divine revelation of the Supreme Absolute Truth

sloka_826.meaning: by the ancient mahājans, great saintly persons, like Janaka and others

sloka_831.meaning: attachment to the fruits of action
and content in the eternal bliss within

sloka_827.meaning: what is karma and akarma, action and inaction

sloka_850.meaning: The faithful (having faith in the Lord’s words or the scriptures)

sloka_817.meaning: in My own divine nature of truth, auspiciousness, and beauty

sloka_822.meaning: all types of men (on all paths)

sloka_839.meaning: perform ‘knowledge-sacrifice’ in the form of singing and introspectively studying the Vedas
perform sacrifice in the form of penance (such as Chāndrāyaṇ, etc.)
perform sacrifice in the form of the eightfold mystic yoga practice

sloka_832.meaning: and a renouncer of all possessiveness and acquisition

sloka_812.meaning: yoga of knowledge (jñāna-yoga) achieved by selfless action

sloka_841.meaning: Purified of sin by the performance of sacrifice
they enjoy the neatarean remnants of sacrifice
